summary: Backend-only testing for Polyglot AI Translator (Pingo-style). 19 pytest cases written covering languages, translate (en→ko/fr/ar-ma + auto→es), image OCR+translate, TTS, transcribe (whisper-1), chat session persistence/clear, and history CRUD with favorite. All 19 tests pass after one backend fix (transcribe was passing a str filepath into OpenAI/litellm which expects file-handle/PathLike). Note: intermittent 500s observed when many Gemini calls run back-to-back due to an Emergent LLM Key per-window budget — endpoint code is correct.

minor.issue: Intermittent 500 'litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0036, Max budget: 0.001' when many LLM calls run in rapid succession. Appears to be an EMERGENT_LLM_KEY per-window budget throttle, not a code defect. Re-running after a short wait succeeds. Consider exposing a friendlier 429-style error in the API and/or implementing simple retry-with-backoff on the server side.

action_items: (Optional) Surface LLM budget/rate-limit errors as 429 with a clear message instead of opaque 500, and consider a small server-side retry on litellm BadRequestError 'Budget has been exceeded'.
Add a UX-level rate-limit notice in the frontend chat/translate flows so users aren't confused by transient 500s while the key budget window resets.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/transcribe was passing the temp file path as a str into emergentintegrations OpenAISpeechToText.transcribe(file=...), which litellm/openai-python rejects ('Expected entry at file to be bytes, an io.IOBase instance, PathLike or a tuple but received <class str>'). FIXED by opening the file handle (`with open(tmp.name, 'rb') as fh: kwargs['file']=fh`).
Single 510-line server.py is acceptable for current MVP but will grow quickly — when image/voice features expand, split into routers (translate, chat, history, audio) for maintainability.
Chat replays the last 10 turns by stringifying them into the prompt. This works but doesn't leverage LlmChat's native multi-turn state. Functional and correct; just note for future refactor.
All Mongo reads correctly project out `_id` ({'_id': 0}) — good.
All env vars (MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY) read from environment — no hardcoded secrets/URLs in code.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (1 fix: opened audio file handle for whisper transcription)
seed_data_creation: Tests create their own ephemeral history items prefixed with TEST_ and clean them up via DELETE. Chat session ids are TEST_session_<uuid> and cleaned via DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL = EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Run: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Image OCR test renders a PNG with PIL (DejaVuSans-Bold). Transcribe test reuses /api/tts output as input — depends on TTS working. If you see batch 500s mentioning 'Budget has been exceeded', wait 15–30s and rerun individual tests — it's an LLM key budget window, not a regression.
rca_of_issue: Issue: POST /api/transcribe → 500 'Expected entry at file to be bytes, an io.IOBase instance, PathLike or a tuple but received <class str>'. RCA: server.py wrote audio bytes to a NamedTemporaryFile and then passed `tmp.name` (a str) into emergentintegrations OpenAISpeechToText.transcribe(file=...). Internally litellm/openai-python's multipart upload requires bytes / io.IOBase / PathLike / tuple. A str path is not auto-coerced. Fix: open the temp file in binary mode and pass the file handle. Verified by re-running TestTranscribe — passes and returns a whisper-1 transcript that contains >=2 of the expected keywords from the synthesized TTS audio.
